name: Yashika Sapra
about:
I build intelligent systems from neurons to APIs.

**Three things:**
1. I think like a systems architect, not a learner → I design for production: scalability, monitoring, failure handling.

2. I ship the full stack → ML models + APIs + frontends + DevOps. I don't stop at notebooks.

3. I solve real problems → Multi-agent AI for job automation. IoT networks handling 1000+ events/sec. Data systems processing millions.

**Currently:** B.Tech CS (AI/ML/IoT specialization, 2023–2027) at GLA University

**Previously:** 5+ hackathon wins. 15+ production projects. Multiple technical competitions. Shipped systems used by 50+ beta users.

I obsess over impact metrics: latency, throughput, reliability. Every system I build has real numbers attached.

education.details: Specialization: Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning & IoT

workshops.title: Incept at GLA University
workshops.description: Attended an enriching session organized by Google Developers Group GLA University on real-world tech careers, project impact, and problem-solving with speakers from industry.

workshops.title: Generative AI & Agentic AI Workshop
workshops.description:
🚀 Hands-on Experience in Generative AI & Agentic AI
Recently, I had the opportunity to attend an intensive workshop on Generative AI and Agentic AI, and it turned out to be a highly practical and insightful experience.
🔹 What I explored during the workshop:
🧠 Fundamentals of Generative AI models and how they work in real-world applications
🤖 Concepts of Agentic AI — building systems that can think, decide, and act autonomously
🛠️ Hands-on experience with multiple tools, libraries, and AI frameworks
⚙️ Built and experimented with AI agents and generative models
🔗 Learned how to integrate AI into workflows for solving real problems
🔹 Key Takeaways:
Understanding how to move from static AI models → intelligent agents
Importance of structured prompts, context handling, and orchestration
Practical exposure to building end-to-end AI-powered solutions
A big thank you to Tarang Nigam Sir for conducting such an engaging and knowledge-packed workshop. The way complex concepts were broken down into practical implementations made it extremely valuable.
💡 This experience has strengthened my interest in building AI-driven systems and autonomous solutions.
